title: Extract relaxed schema constant and harden test setup in reconciliation tests
analysis: Two tests in test_reconciliation.py (test_null_started_at_with_completed_at_sets_duration_none at line 110 and test_null_started_at_does_not_affect_other_rows at line 166) derive a relaxed schema via `RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA.replace('started_at TEXT NOT NULL', 'started_at TEXT')`. This is fragile: if the schema constant in conftest.py changes formatting (e.g. extra spaces, different casing, column reordering), the replace silently returns the original strict schema, causing an IntegrityError when inserting NULL started_at values — a misleading failure. The fix: (1) define RELAXED_RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA in conftest.py alongside RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA, (2) add a module-level assertion that the two schemas differ as expected, (3) replace the inline .replace() calls with imports of the new constant. The make_recon_db helper already accepts a `schema` kwarg, so no changes needed there.

prerequisites.description: Verify existing tests pass before refactoring — run pytest on dashboard/tests/test_reconciliation.py to establish green baseline

steps.description: Write test_relaxed_schema_constant_exists_and_differs in test_reconciliation.py: import RELAXED_RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA from conftest, assert it exists, assert 'started_at TEXT NOT NULL' is NOT in the relaxed schema, assert 'started_at TEXT' IS in the relaxed schema, and assert the two schemas differ only in the NOT NULL constraint on started_at (i.e. relaxed_schema.replace('started_at TEXT', 'started_at TEXT NOT NULL') == RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA). This test will fail because the constant doesn't exist yet.

Define RELAXED_RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA in conftest.py right after RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA. Construct it as a copy of RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA with 'started_at TEXT NOT NULL' replaced by 'started_at TEXT'. Add a module-level assert that validates 'started_at TEXT NOT NULL' appears in RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA (so the derivation can't silently fail). Also add a comment explaining the relationship. This makes step-1's test pass.
Verify both existing null-started_at tests still pass with the current inline .replace() approach — this confirms the new constant is compatible. Run the two affected tests to confirm green.

Refactor test_null_started_at_with_completed_at_sets_duration_none (line ~110): remove inline RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA import and .replace() call, import RELAXED_RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA from tests.conftest instead, pass it directly to make_recon_db's schema kwarg.

Refactor test_null_started_at_does_not_affect_other_rows (line ~166): same change — remove inline RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA import and .replace() call, import RELAXED_RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA from tests.conftest, pass to make_recon_db's schema kwarg.
Run full test_reconciliation.py test suite to confirm all tests pass after the refactoring, especially the two refactored null-started_at tests and the new schema constant test.

design_decisions.decision: Derive RELAXED_RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA from RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA at module level rather than defining it as a separate string literal
design_decisions.rationale: Keeping it derived ensures it stays in sync automatically. The module-level assert guards against the derivation silently failing if the source string changes.

design_decisions.decision: Add a module-level assert in conftest.py that 'started_at TEXT NOT NULL' exists in RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA
design_decisions.rationale: This is the key hardening: if the schema string changes such that the substring match would fail, we get an immediate ImportError/AssertionError at test collection time rather than a misleading IntegrityError at test runtime.

design_decisions.decision: Place the schema constant test as a standalone test function (not inside a class) at the module level of test_reconciliation.py
design_decisions.rationale: Schema fixture correctness is cross-cutting, not specific to any one query function's test class. A top-level test makes it easy to find and signals its infrastructure-validation purpose.

reuse.how: The new RELAXED_RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA is derived from it via .replace(), but now done once at module scope with a guard assertion
Already accepts a `schema` kwarg — no changes needed. Both refactored tests pass RELAXED_RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA through this parameter.
